templateGuide.description: Guide for AI Plugin Development Service to generate plugins using _ai_template.js

{{PLUGIN_TITLE}}.description: Human-readable display title for menu and headers
rules: used in UI menu and page header

{{PLUGIN_DESCRIPTION}}.example: Get weather forecasts and current conditions for any location

{{PLUGIN_COMMANDS}}.description: JSON array of command objects with examples for vector matching
{{PLUGIN_COMMANDS}}.example: [{"command": "current", "description": "Get current weather for any location", "usage": "current({ location: 'New York' })", "examples": ["what's the weather in New York", "current weather for NYC", "how's the weather in manhattan", "is it raining in new york", "temperature in NYC right now"]}, {"command": "forecast", "description": "Get multi-day weather forecast", "usage": "forecast({ location: 'London', days: 5 })", "examples": ["weather forecast for London", "what's the weather like in London next week", "5 day forecast for UK", "will it rain in London tomorrow", "weekend weather in London"]}]

exampleUsage.instruction: To create a weather plugin, replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} markers with appropriate code
exampleUsage.apiDiscovery: First discover the API structure and authentication method
exampleUsage.commandMapping: Map API endpoints to natural language commands
exampleUsage.errorHandling: Add try-catch blocks and meaningful error messages
exampleUsage.caching: Implement caching for frequently accessed data
exampleUsage.vectorIntents: Commands array is automatically indexed for vector search

vectorIntentSystem.overview: The commands array is automatically indexed by the vector intent detection system
vectorIntentSystem.automatic: When a plugin is enabled, its intents are indexed. When disabled, they are removed
vectorIntentSystem.structure: Each command must have: command, description, usage, and optionally examples array
vectorIntentSystem.examples: The examples array should contain natural language variations users might say
vectorIntentSystem.bestPractice: Include 3-5 diverse examples per command for better NLP matching

helperFiles.when: Create helper files when the plugin exceeds 500 lines or has distinct feature sets
helperFiles.location: Place in same directory as main plugin: src/api/plugins/
helperFiles.exports: Export named objects/functions that the main plugin imports

examples.management: export const DataManagement = { async create() {...}, async update() {...} }

pluginSettings.overview: Use PluginSettings for persistent configuration with built-in caching
benefits: Better performance for frequently accessed settings

examples.apiTokens: Cache API tokens with custom TTL

pluginAPI.overview: Plugins are called via the generic plugin API endpoint
pluginAPI.authentication: Requires Bearer token or X-API-Key header

credentialSystem.overview: Plugins can require API keys that users configure via the web UI

loadCredentials.description: Load credentials from database (encrypted) or env vars as fallback
loadCredentials.usage: const creds = await this.loadCredentials([{ key: 'apiKey', envVar: 'MY_API_KEY', required: true }])
loadCredentials.returns: Object with credential values, throws if required creds missing

checkCredentials.description: Check if credentials are configured without loading values
checkCredentials.usage: const status = await this.checkCredentials([{ key: 'apiKey', envVar: 'MY_API_KEY', required: true }])

credentialSystem.webUIIntegration: Users can configure credentials in Plugins tab > Settings modal

basePluginMethods.overview: Methods inherited from BasePlugin - available in all plugins via 'this.'

executeCommand.description: Execute shell commands with optional approval

storeMemory.description: Store data in agent's persistent memory

processWithAI.description: Process text using current AI provider
processWithAI.usage: const response = await this.processWithAI('Summarize this text: ...')

getConfig.description: Get plugin config value with default

setConfig.description: Set plugin config value (emits config:changed event)

customHTTPRoutes.overview: Plugins can define custom HTTP endpoints via getRoutes()
notes: Use for webhooks, streaming endpoints, or custom API formats
Standard plugin actions should use execute() instead

bestPractices: Keep command names short and intuitive
Write detailed descriptions for vector matching
Include usage examples in command definitions
Cache API responses when appropriate using node-cache
Log errors with context for debugging
Store sensitive data (API keys) via loadCredentials/credential system
Always return { success: true/false, data/error } from execute()
Use this.logger for logging (auto-creates plugin-specific log file)
Validate all user input with validateParams()

projectUtilities.overview: Available utility modules - use these instead of creating new implementations
projectUtilities.importPaths: Plugins are in src/api/plugins/ so use ../../utils/ for utility imports

nodeCache.usage: const cache = new NodeCache({ stdTTL: 300, checkperiod: 60 });
nodeCache.note: Use for temporary in-memory caching. For persistent config, use PluginSettings instead.

functions.retryOperation: retryOperation(asyncFn, options) - retry with exponential backoff and circuit breaker

retryUtils.usage: await retryOperation(() => axios.get(url), { retries: 3, context: 'API call' })
retryUtils.note: DO NOT use retry() or retryWithCircuitBreaker() - they do not exist

functions.safeJsonParse: safeJsonParse(text, defaultValue) - parse JSON without throwing

encryption.usage: Use for storing sensitive API keys or tokens

rateLimit.usage: Apply to plugin routes that need rate limiting

warnings: DO NOT import logger directly - use this.logger (inherited from BasePlugin)
DO NOT create new Winston instances
DO NOT use non-existent functions like retry() or retryWithCircuitBreaker()
DO NOT import Agenda directly - scheduled tasks are managed by TaskScheduler service

webUIIntegration.overview: Plugins can add menu items and custom pages to the web dashboard

menuItems.description: Add a getUIConfig() method to register a menu item

menuItem.title: Display title shown in sidebar menu
menuItem.order: Numeric position in menu (lower = higher in list)

returns.hasUI: Boolean - true if plugin has a web UI

pageContent.description: Add a getUIContent() method to return the HTML for your plugin's page
pageContent.returns: HTML string - the content for your plugin's tab/page
notes: Content is loaded when user clicks the menu item
Include inline <style> tags for plugin-specific CSS (auto-prefixed)
Include inline <script> tags for plugin JavaScript
Use class names like 'plugin-header', 'plugin-content' for consistent styling
Access API via fetch('/api/plugin', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ plugin: 'name', action: 'action' }) })

menuOrderGuide.description: Suggested order ranges to keep menus organized

themeCompatibility.description: Follow these guidelines for consistent theming

testTemplate.overview: Tests are in tests/plugins/ directory, one test file per plugin
requirements: Test each action using its ACTUAL command name from commands array
NEVER use placeholder action names like 'validAction'

structure.pluginStructure: Test plugin has required properties (name, description, commands)
structure.actionTests: Test each action from commands array with success and error cases
structure.errorHandling: Test unknown actions and API failures

commonMistakes.description: Common errors to AVOID when generating plugins and tests

criticalErrors.mistake: Using placeholder action names in tests
criticalErrors.correct: plugin.execute({ action: 'getLocation' }) // Use actual command from commands array
criticalErrors.why: Tests must use actual action names that exist in the plugin's commands

criticalErrors.mistake: Wrong import paths from plugins directory
criticalErrors.why: Plugins are in src/api/plugins/, utils are in src/utils/ - need ../../
Use getService() to access scheduler, don't call non-existent plugin methods

criticalErrors.wrong: async getCurrentData() { return null; // TODO implement }
criticalErrors.correct: async getCurrentData() { const response = await axios.get(...); return response.data; }
criticalErrors.why: All methods must have working implementations, not stubs

criticalErrors.correct: // Only import what you actually use

availableFunctions.note: Do NOT import functions that don't exist - check the actual utility files

testActionNames.rule: ALWAYS use actual action names from the plugin's commands array
testActionNames.how: Look at the plugin's this.commands array - each object has a 'command' property
testActionNames.example: If plugin has commands: [{command: 'getLocation'}, {command: 'getOwnLocation'}], tests must use 'getLocation' or 'getOwnLocation', NEVER 'validAction'
